evals.prompt: hey can you enrich our record for Brightpath Consulting? they're a Tier 1 account but the data is embarassingly stale -- last touched maybe 14 months ago. Main contact is David Hargreaves, he was Head of Ops when we last spoke but I think hes moved. Also pretty sure their headcount jumped after that Series B last year. Their rep is Jess Moreno and they're in an active nurture sequence right now. Oh and Jess manually entered the direct phone number herself from a conference badge so dont mess with that. Need this cleaned up before our QBR Thursday.
evals.expected_output: A CRM enrichment report covering both contact-level and account-level fields for Brightpath Consulting and David Hargreaves, with proper handling of the manually-entered phone field, the stale LinkedIn concern, the active sequence, and rep notification for Jess Moreno.
expectations: To prevent wasted outreach to a stale contact, the output flags David Hargreaves for potential role change (no LinkedIn updates in >12 months) rather than assuming he is still Head of Ops at Brightpath Consulting -- a wrong-title email tanks response rates

To protect revenue on an active deal, the output does NOT overwrite the manually-entered direct phone number -- it explicitly flags it as a human-entered field and defers to Jess Moreno as record owner before any change, since reps trust CRM data they entered themselves

To protect the active nurture sequence from breaking, the output does NOT remove David Hargreaves from the sequence due to any email change -- instead it specifies pausing the sequence and alerting Jess Moreno to verify new contact details first, preventing a live prospect from going dark

To enable accurate segmentation and lead routing after the Series B, account-level enrichment addresses Brightpath Consulting headcount with a flag if the change exceeds 20%, plus the Series B funding captured as a news event -- these fields drive tier classification and ICP scoring

To ensure the QBR uses reliable data, the output includes a DATA QUALITY SCORE as a percentage of enriched records now complete, plus a RECORDS WITH MISSING MANDATORY FIELDS section listing any fields still unfilled -- giving the rep a clear picture of what is verified vs. what still needs manual work

To drive accurate pipeline forecasting, the output covers all six contact-level mandatory fields (full name, current title, current company, work email, direct phone, LinkedIn URL) and all five account-level mandatory fields (legal name, revenue estimate, headcount, headquarters, website) -- incomplete records create routing errors and missed handoffs

evals.prompt: We need a bulk enrichment across all our HOT leads in the logistics vertical -- about 40 contacts across 12 accounts. Specifically I want to catch anyone who changed roles in the last 90 days because we've been getting bounced emails from at least 3 of them (Tom Brierly at NexaFreight, someone at Cargoline Solutions, and I think Maria Escobar at PeakPort). Two of those accounts -- NexaFreight and PeakPort -- have active deals at proposal stage with our rep Amir Khalil so be careful with those. Also pull any new hiring signals in their engineering/IT departments because we're pitching our API integration product. I need this by end of day ideally, formatting for our weekly sales standup tmrw morning.
evals.expected_output: A bulk CRM enrichment report across the HOT logistics segment covering 40 contacts / 12 accounts, with career change detection, bounced email resolution, hiring signal intelligence, and special handling for the two active-deal accounts.
expectations: To stop wasting outreach on bounced contacts, the ROLE CHANGES DETECTED section specifically flags Tom Brierly at NexaFreight, the Cargoline Solutions contact, and Maria Escobar at PeakPort with old-to-new role transitions and marks each for rep review and re-routing -- every bounced email in an active sequence burns sender reputation

To protect two deals at proposal stage from data disruption, the output explicitly states that Amir Khalil is notified before any record updates are applied to NexaFreight and PeakPort -- silently changing contact data on a deal at proposal stage can derail a close

To prevent false confidence in contact data, bounced email addresses are NOT marked as verified without an actual deliverability check -- the output distinguishes between emails that have been checked vs. those pending verification, because sending to an unverified 'updated' email is worse than knowing it bounces

To create pipeline for the API integration product pitch, account-level intelligence includes hiring signals specifically in engineering/IT departments with reference to recent job postings, plus tech stack changes visible in those postings -- these are the buying signals that justify moving an account to active outreach

To give the sales standup actionable next steps, the output ends with a DATA QUALITY SCORE percentage and provides specific next steps for each flagged record (who to contact, what to verify, which sequences to pause) rather than generic recommendations

To enable accurate segment-level pipeline reporting, the enrichment report scope reflects both the ~40 contacts and ~12 accounts in the HOT logistics vertical, with structured sections for CHANGES MADE, ROLE CHANGES DETECTED, NEW HOT SIGNALS IDENTIFIED, and RECORDS WITH MISSING MANDATORY FIELDS

evals.prompt: trigger enrichment -- we just got a content download notification from someone at Vantage Health Systems, they grabbed our whitepaper on clinical workflow automation. The form submission says: Priya Nandakumar, Clinical Operations Director, priya.n@vantagehealth.co.uk, no phone given. We've never had them in the CRM before so this is net-new. I want full contact enrichment plus account enrichment on Vantage Health Systems. Check if they're a fit -- we target mid-market healthcare orgs 200-2000 headcount in UK. Also see if there's anyone else at Vantage we should be talking to in IT or procurement. This should feed into lead scoring after.
evals.expected_output: A trigger-based CRM enrichment report for the net-new contact Priya Nandakumar and account Vantage Health Systems, with full mandatory and intelligence field population, ICP fit assessment against mid-market UK healthcare criteria, and multi-threading recommendations.
expectations: To prevent pipeline leakage from slow follow-up on inbound intent, the output identifies this as trigger-based enrichment from a content download event and references the 24-hour trigger-based enrichment SLA -- a whitepaper download is a buying signal that decays fast

To ensure outreach goes to a valid address, the work email priya.n@vantagehealth.co.uk is listed but NOT marked as verified without a deliverability check, and the missing direct phone is flagged for manual addition -- sending a personalised sequence to a bad email wastes the entire effort

To qualify or disqualify before investing rep time, the account-level enrichment for Vantage Health Systems includes headcount assessed against the 200-2000 mid-market target range and headquarters verified as UK -- these are the two ICP gates that determine whether this lead gets routed to a rep or deprioritised

To enable multi-threading into the buying committee, the report recommends additional contacts at Vantage Health Systems in IT or procurement departments -- single-threaded deals close at half the rate of multi-threaded ones, and enrichment is where thread targets are identified

To create a complete lead record that scores accurately, the output includes a DATA QUALITY SCORE percentage and a RECORDS WITH MISSING MANDATORY FIELDS section listing the missing phone and any other unfilled fields -- lead scoring models that run on incomplete data produce misleading scores

To capture buying intent timing, intelligence fields include the content download as a timing signal, career change flag for Priya Nandakumar (new role vs. established position), and account-level signals (news events, hiring signals, financial signals) with a timing classification of HOT/WARM/WATCH -- these fields drive whether the lead enters a fast or slow sequence
